name: Azerbaijan Premier League
content:
The Azerbaijan Premier League is the highest tier of professional football in Azerbaijan, organized by the Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan. It features the country’s top clubs competing annually for the national championship and qualification to European competitions. The league plays a central role in the development of football in Azerbaijan and has grown in professionalism and visibility over time.

Before the 2006–07 season, the top division was known as the Azerbaijan Higher League. The rebranding marked a structural and organizational shift aimed at modernizing the competition and aligning it more closely with international standards. Since then, the Premier League format has undergone several changes, including variations in the number of participating teams and competition rules.

Historically, clubs like Neftçi PFK and Qarabağ FK have been among the most successful and influential teams in the league. In particular, Qarabağ has gained international recognition through consistent performances in UEFA competitions, reflecting the league’s gradual rise in competitiveness and reputation within European football.

name: Azerbaijan Cup
content:
The Azerbaijan Cup is the main knockout football tournament in Azerbaijan, organized by the Association of Football Federations of Azerbaijan. Founded in its modern form in 1992 after the country gained independence, it brings together clubs from different tiers of Azerbaijani football, with the winner earning qualification for European competitions such as the UEFA Europa Conference League.

The competition follows a knockout format involving teams from both the top-tier Azerbaijan Premier League and lower divisions. Lower-league clubs enter in earlier rounds, while top-flight teams join later stages, with two-legged ties used in the later rounds and a single-match final deciding the champion.

Historically, the cup traces its roots back to a similar tournament held during the Soviet era starting in 1936, though the modern competition has developed separately since independence. Clubs such as Qarabağ FK are among the most successful, reflecting their dominance in domestic football. Despite its importance, the tournament has generally attracted less public attention compared to the national league, leading organizers to experiment with formats to increase interest.
